phrasalVerbs.definition: To become weaker or less common and then disappear completely.
examples: Many traditional crafts are in danger of dying out.
The dinosaurs died out millions of years ago.
Local dialects are slowly dying out in many regions.

phrasalVerbs.definition: To dig holes in an area of land; to find information by searching; to remove something from underground.
examples: Archaeologists dug up ancient artefacts at the site.
The journalist dug up some surprising facts about the company.
We dug up the old pipes to replace them.

phrasalVerbs.definition: To stop being available; to become completely dry.
examples: Investment in the sector has dried up completely.
The river dried up during the severe drought.
Job opportunities have dried up in this industry.

phrasalVerbs.definition: To destroy living things so that most or all of them are dead.
examples: Pollution is killing off marine life in the bay.
The frost killed off most of the crops.
Antibiotics can kill off beneficial bacteria.

phrasalVerbs.definition: To appear or be produced suddenly and quickly.
examples: New restaurants are springing up all over the city.
Problems tend to spring up when you least expect them.
Coffee shops have sprung up on every corner.

fillInExercises.sentence: Many species of wildlife are in danger of ___.
fillInExercises.explanation: 'Die out' means to become extinct or disappear.

fillInExercises.sentence: The researchers ___ up evidence of an ancient settlement.
fillInExercises.explanation: 'Dig up' means to discover through searching or excavation.

fillInExercises.sentence: Funding for the project has completely ___.
fillInExercises.explanation: 'Dry up' means to stop being available.

fillInExercises.sentence: The harsh winter ___ off most of the insects.
fillInExercises.explanation: 'Kill off' means to destroy or eliminate living things.

fillInExercises.sentence: New tech startups are ___ everywhere in the city.
fillInExercises.explanation: 'Spring up' means to appear suddenly and rapidly.

fillInExercises.sentence: Old traditions are slowly ___ out in modern society.

fillInExercises.explanation: 'Kill off' means to destroy or eliminate.

fillInExercises.sentence: Opportunities seem to ___ up when you're prepared.

lines.speaker: Miguel
lines.text: Many endangered species are [dying out|die out] due to habitat loss.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: 'Die out' means to become extinct or disappear.
explanationContent.vocabularyFocus: *die out* — To become weaker or less common and then disappear completely.
explanationContent.sentenceBreakdown: Many endangered species are dying out due to habitat loss.
explanationContent.commonMistake: Do not confuse this with other items from the lesson — here you need *die out* (*die out*).

wrongOptions.dig up: *dig up* (To dig holes in an area of land) does not match the meaning needed in this line.
wrongOptions.dry up: *dry up* (To stop being available) does not match the meaning needed in this line.
wrongOptions.kill off: *kill off* (To destroy living things so that most or all of them are dead) does not match the meaning needed in this line.
wrongOptions.spring up: *spring up* (To appear or be produced suddenly and quickly) does not match the meaning needed in this line.

explanationContent.usefulTip: In conversation, *die out* is often used in informal spoken English.
